options.delta_label_convention: Delta labels (25DC, 25DP, 10DC, 10DP) refer to the BASE currency, USD. 25DC = 25-delta USD call (MXN put); 25DP = 25-delta USD put (MXN call). Strong positive 25d RR (25DC >> 25DP) reflects persistent MXN crash risk premium — USD calls are significantly more expensive.
options.liquid_strikes: ATM through 25D; 10D strikes are liquid in 1M-3M
options.illiquidity_note: Vol thins beyond 1Y; 10D strikes are wide market beyond 3M

notes: MXN rates (Banxico) are well above USD rates; long USD pays significant carry
Historically one of the highest-carry EM pairs; carry can exceed 5% annually
Negative carry for long USD but positive for short USD — popular carry trade destination

market_structure.skew_driver: political risk premium, nearshoring uncertainty, US tariff risk, oil price sensitivity
market_structure.liquidity_profile: good in core tenors given high carry trader interest; spreads widen in stress

risk_notes: US trade policy: USDMXN is extremely sensitive to US tariff announcements; gap risk is high
Banxico independence: political interference risk with Banxico rate decisions
Oil sensitivity: Mexico is an oil exporter; lower oil prices weaken MXN
Nearshoring flows: significant corporate FX demand from manufacturing relocation to Mexico
Carry unwind: in risk-off episodes, USDMXN can gap 3-5% in a session as carry positions unwind
Deliverable: physical settlement; requires custody and settlement infrastructure
